perturbation.original_text: The Recipient ag rees to keep the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information confidential and to protect the confidentiality of such Confidential Information with the same degree of care with which it protects the confidentiality of its own confidential information, but i n no event with less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees to keep the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information confidential; however, the Recipient is not required to take any specific measures to protect it beyond what is required by general business practices.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text weakens the confidentiality obligation. Originally, the Recipient had to protect the information with at least a 'reasonable degree of care.' The modified text removes this specific standard, implying a potentially lower standard based on 'general business practices', thus directly contradicting the intended rigor of confidentiality as initially agreed upon. Now it is not clear if there is a breach of contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. All Confidential Information disclosed to the Recipient will be used solely for the Business Purpose and for no other purpose whatsoever. The Recipient agrees to keep the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information confidential; however, the Recipient is not required to take any specific measures to protect it beyond what is required by general business practices. Recipient may disclose Confidential Information only to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors on a need -to-know basis, and only if such employees, agents, consultants and contractors have executed appropriate written agreements with Recipient sufficient to enable Recipient to enforce all the provisions of this Agreement. Recipient shall not make any copies of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information except as needed for the Business Purp ose. At the request of Disclosing Party, Recipient shall return to Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including any copies thereof) or certify the destruction thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the confidentiality obligation by removing the 'reasonable degree of care' standard and replacing it with 'general business practices.' This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text, which includes the changed text, discusses keeping confidential information confidential and only disclosing it on a need-to-know basis with agreements sufficient to enforce provisions. The reduced standard of care now contradicts the implicit expectation of a higher level of protection for Confidential Information, thus creating inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: At the request of Disclosing Party, Recipient shall return to Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including any copies thereof) or certify the destruction thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: At the request of the Disclosing Party, the Recipient may, at its sole discretion, return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party (including any copies thereof) or certify the destruction thereof, only if the Disclosing Party pays for all associated costs.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates the return or destruction of confidential information upon request. The modified version makes it discretionary ('may') and introduces a condition (Disclosing Party pays all costs). This introduces an in-text contradiction because the Recipient now has the option to refuse the return/destruction unless compensated, undermining the Disclosing Party's right to reclaim their information. The original text makes it compulsory, but the modified version, introduce costs to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. All Confidential Information disclosed to the Recipient will be used solely for the Business Purpose and for no other purpose whatsoever. The Recipient ag rees to keep the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information confidential; however, the Recipient is not required to take any specific measures to protect it beyond what is required by general business practices. Recipient may disclose Confidential Information only to its employees, agents, consultants and contractors on a need -to-know basis, and only if such employees, agents, consultants and contractors have executed appropriate written agreements with Recipient sufficient to enable Recipient to enforce all the provisions of this Agreement. Recipient shall not make any copies of Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information except as needed for the Business Purp ose. At the request of Disclosing Party, the Recipient may, at its sole discretion, return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party (including any copies thereof) or certify the destruction thereof, only if the Disclosing Party pays for all associated costs.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the return or destruction of confidential information upon request of the Disclosing Party. The changed text introduces a contingency by making it discretionary ('may') and conditional ('only if the Disclosing Party pays for all associated costs'). This directly contradicts the original obligation, creating a clear in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which now contains the modified clause, because it undermines the Disclosing Party's right to reclaim their information freely. The Recipient now has the option to refuse the return/destruction unless compensated.

perturbation.original_text: ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROV IDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING NON - INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS OR ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.changed_text: ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROV IDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY WARRANTS THAT IT HAS THE RIGHT TO DISCLOSE THIS INFORMATION AND THAT IT IS ACCURATE AND COMPLETE.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction concerning the warranties associated with the confidential information. The original text explicitly states that there are *no* warranties regarding accuracy, completeness, or non-infringement. The modified text then introduces a warranty that the information *is* accurate and complete, and that the Disclosing Party has the right to disclose it. This fundamental conflict creates uncertainty about what warranties, if any, apply to the disclosed information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. All right title and interest in and to the Confidenti al Information shall remain with Disclosing Party or its licensors. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets of Disclosing Party. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROV IDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY WARRANTS THAT IT HAS THE RIGHT TO DISCLOSE THIS INFORMATION AND THAT IT IS ACCURATE AND COMPLETE.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly disclaims any warranties regarding the accuracy, completeness, or non-infringement of the confidential information. The changed text directly contradicts this by introducing a warranty that the information *is* accurate and complete and that the Disclosing Party has the right to disclose it. This creates a direct conflict with the statement in the contradicted text also, as it contains the changed text. Therefore, the changed text is inconsistent with the original agreement regarding warranties, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as the Disclosing Party cannot both warrant and disclaim warranties for the same information, as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall control in the event of a conflict with any other agreement betwee n the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall control in the event of a conflict with any other agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, unless the other agreement is in writing and signed by both parties, in which case that agreement shall control.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause establishes the NDA as the governing agreement in case of conflicts. The modified clause introduces an exception: if another agreement is in writing and signed, *that* agreement takes precedence. This creates an in-text contradiction and undermines the certainty provided by the original clause. If a future conflict arises, it's now uncertain whether the NDA or the new agreement will govern the situation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. The validity, construction and enforceability of this Agreement shall be gover ned in all respects by the law of the state of Colorado . This Agreement may not be amended except in writing signed by a duly authorized representative of the respective Parties. This Agreement shall control in the event of a conflict with any other agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, unless the other agreement is in writing and signed by both parties, in which case that agreement shall control. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written. Disclosing Party: Company: _______________________ Name: __________________________ Role: ___________________________ Phone Number: __________________ Signature: ___________________________ ____ The Recipient : Company: _____ Pitch Deck Fire ______ Name: _______ Stacie Shaw __________ Role: _________ Founder ___________ Phone Number: __ _214-770-9216 ____ Signature: _______________________________
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement shall control in the event of a conflict with any other agreement. The changed text introduces an exception to this rule, stating that if the other agreement is in writing and signed by both parties, then that agreement shall control. The contradicted text also states that the agreement shall control in the event of a conflict with any other agreement, unless the other agreement is in writing and signed by both parties, in which case that agreement shall control. Therefore there is a contradiction because the change impacts the enforcement and interpretation of which agreement controls if there is conflict.
